hypothesis: Enhancing selectivity margin by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 to better complement SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 interactions, and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 to avoid off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 hydrophobic pockets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid, but the focus must shift to improving selectivity by disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. This will require targeted mutations to reduce SSTR3/SSTR5 binding.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in conserved-pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
Introducing polar or hydrophobic mutations at these positions can break conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore core (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
